channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rep Gimenez warns China is 'greatest threat' to US, Trump admin will 'project strength' to CCP
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Rep. Carlos Gimenez of the Select Committee on the CCP says President-elect Trump will bring “peace through strength, not peace through appeasement" when dealing with China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump leaves China guessing what his next move is with unusual inauguration invitation
item.description: President-elect Trump took China by surprise when he invited President Xi Jinping to his upcoming inauguration, a friendly gesture ahead of a widely expected trade war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump appoints longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as special missions envoy
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump announced Saturday that he's picked longtime foreign policy adviser Richard Grenell to serve as an envoy for special missions, tasking him with helping the incoming administration deal with some of the toughest foreign policy challenges.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Richard Grenell as presidential envoy for special missions, Edward S. Walsh as Ireland ambassador
item.description: Trump named Richard Grenell, former acting director of national intelligence, as an envoy for special missions and Edward S. Walsh as his pick to serve as ambassador to Ireland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what to know about the US push for stability in post-Assad Syria
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken has wrapped up perhaps his last Middle East visit as America’s top diplomat, with the aim of preventing Syria from spiraling out of control after the sudden ouster of President Bashar Assad. Blinken was one of several senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump leans on czars for key policy areas
item.description: Trump plans to lean on appointed czars to carry out his plans in key policy areas, specifically when it comes to immigration and energy production.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces more nominations, including Devin Nunes, Troy Edgar and Bill White
item.description: Trump nominated Truth Social CEO Devin Nunes as chairman of his Intelligence Advisory Board, Troy Edgar as deputy secretary of Homeland Security and Bill White as ambassador to Belgium.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominees should ‘steer clear’ of undermining polio vaccine, McConnell says
item.description: Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell, a childhood polio survivor, said any of President-elect Donald Trump’s nominees seeking confirmation should “steer clear” of efforts to discredit the polio vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump, close allies attend Army-Navy rivalry game
item.description: As national concerns about the drones spotted on the East Coast continue to mount, President-elect Trump is attending the Army-Navy game.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: Pelosi, 84, “is well on the mend,” said Ian Krager, a spokesman for the California Democrat, in a statement.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nancy Pelosi announces major surgery after fall in Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Rep. Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., underwent successful hip surgery overseas after her fall in Luxembourg, her office revealed in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: European countries put freeze on Syrian asylum claims after Assad's fall, unclear if US will follow
item.description: European countries are freezing asylum cases from Syrian migrants after the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad, raising questions about how the U.S. will handle claims.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny, acquitted in NYC subway chokehold, will join Trump's suite at football game
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Daniel Penny, a military veteran who choked an agitated New York subway rider and was acquitted of homicide this week, has been invited by Vice President-elect JD Vance to join Donald Trump's suite at the Army-Navy football game on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raskin demands FBI vetting of Trump nominees, details on Epshteyn
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.) on Friday made a series of demands of the Trump transition team, asking for assurances President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees will undergo FBI vetting while also requesting details about aide Boris Epshteyn’s alleged profiting from promoting those seeking new posts.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi hospitalized in Luxembourg after sustaining injury from fall on stairs
item.description: Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) was hospitalized for evaluation in Luxembourg after falling on a set of stairs and sustaining an injury during a trip with a congressional delegation.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: US updates a science and technology pact with China to reflect growing rivalry and security threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. has updated a decades-old science and technology agreement with China to reflect their growing rivalry for technological dominance.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gabbard struggles to woo senators ahead of confirmation fight
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard is struggling through her meetings with senators this week, sources told The Hill, highlighting the difficult path she faces in winning confirmation to be director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration makes final diplomatic push for stability across a turbulent Mideast
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — Top U.S. officials were in the Middle East on Thursday, pushing for stability in Syria and an end to Israel’s 14-month war in the Gaza Strip in a last-ditch diplomatic push by the outgoing Biden administration before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in a few weeks.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to next month's inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is breaking with precedent in inviting a foreign head of state to his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year and rings the New York Stock Exchange's opening bell
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its person of the year.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Trump mass deportation threats infuriate immigrant advocates
item.description: President-elect Trump’s pledge to enact mass deportations and end birthright citizenship, potentially affecting millions of U.S. citizens, is raising alarm bells among immigrant communities who fear an overzealous Trump administration could destroy countless families.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers edge closer to deal on government funding
item.description: Lawmakers say they are edging closer to a deal to keep the government's lights on in nine days but are still trying to work out differences over disaster aid and potential add-ons to the bill. There’s some optimism in the Capitol that bill text could arrive as soon as this weekend as the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's withdrawn nominees latest in a long line of Cabinet members who never were
item.description: Many presidents face failed Cabinet picks so when former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration for President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet, it wasn't unprecedented, though Trump's style has been.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: New Yorkers could receive 'inflation refund' check from state: Here's how much
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Millions of New Yorkers would receive hundreds of dollars in an “inflation refund” payment from the state next year under a new proposal from Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, has promised to deliver a massive reform of a bureau he sees as among the agencies that have “weaponized the government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bidders are back in court battling over the auction of Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: A U.S. bankruptcy judge is hearing arguments for and against selling the show to The Onion, the satirical news site named the winning bidder. Host Alex Jones says the auction was rigged.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News
